$comment: DECLASSIFIED registry — single source of truth for every game, resource, and nav link. To add a game: add one entry to games[], then run tools/build-site.py. Do not hand-edit the generated regions in HTML.

site.name: DECLASSIFIED
site.author: Sean McKendry
site.tagline: Fact-check the record. The walk from headline to source, made playable.

games.title: The Outsider
games.blurb: Campaign emergence, birtherism, crowd size claims, personal attacks on judges. The foundational Phillips patterns.

games.title: The Transition
games.blurb: Post-election claims, inauguration attendance, early executive orders, media attacks. Institutional delegitimization escalates.

games.title: The Reckoning
games.blurb: January 6, the Big Lie, Mar-a-Lago, presidential immunity, and the 2024 campaign. Two new patterns: Victim Narrative and Preemptive Exoneration.

games.title: The Return
games.blurb: Second term claims, Schedule F, tariff misinformation, Benkler Calibration (assess societal impact independent of truth value).

games.title: The Crisis
games.blurb: Constitutional and institutional claims, judicial attacks, executive overreach. The most advanced claims. Includes Benkler Calibration.

games.title: Pro Se Litigation
games.blurb: Fact-check claims about federal civil rights litigation (McKendry v. PNC Bank). Advanced evidence assessment. No time limit.

resources.label: Glossary
resources.blurb: Every pattern, scale, and term — the Phillips Pattern Library and the Bogost Citation Scale, defined.

resources.label: Lesson Mode
resources.blurb: How the Record works — the fact-checking engine shown in the open. The empty dossier is the point: no claim on assertion alone.

resources.label: Current Events: The Epstein Files
resources.blurb: Lesson Mode, applied to a live story: how to read the actual primary documents without getting played — and why named ≠ accused ≠ guilty.

resources.label: Current Events: Project 2025
resources.blurb: Lesson Mode: it's a public 900-page document. Where to find it, how to read it, and how to cite the page instead of the meme — from either side.

resources.label: Current Events: Christian Nationalism
resources.blurb: Lesson Mode: what the term actually means — the political ideology vs. the faith, how scholars measure it, and that many Christians themselves oppose it. Sourced; read it yourself.

resources.label: Current Events: The Murdoch Media Empire
resources.blurb: Lesson Mode: read a media empire by its documented record (the $787.5M Dominion settlement, UK phone-hacking, the holdings) — plus a media-literacy lens to run on EVERY outlet, including your own. Cite the record; call no one a cult.

resources.label: Current Events: The Clintons
resources.blurb: Lesson Mode: Bill & Hillary by the documented record — impeachment & acquittal, the 1990s policy ledger, the email investigation, 2016 — graded without the halo or the conspiracy. Cite the source.

resources.label: Current Events: The Pardon Power
resources.blurb: Lesson Mode: what the pardon power is (Article II), why the record is public, and a checklist to track ANY pardon — incl. January 6 — to its primary source. Documents the record, assigns no shame, names no one to be hated.

resources.label: Current Events: MAHA, Graded
resources.blurb: Lesson Mode: Make America Healthy Again, claim by claim — what the science backs (ultra-processed foods) vs. debunked detours (vaccine-autism, fluoride), and the report caught faking its citations. Right diagnosis, forged homework. Live-sourced.

resources.label: Current Events: MAGA, Graded
resources.blurb: Lesson Mode: Make America Great Again, claim by claim — real grievances (economic dislocation) graded apart from disproven claims (2020 ‘stolen’, Jan 6 ‘peaceful’). Both traps named; same engine as MAHA. Grade the claim, not the jersey.
